nanoracks.name: NanoRacks (Voyager Space)
nanoracks.notes: ISS-based deployment. Orbit: 51.6° inclination, 400–420 km. No hazardous materials. Battery energy limits apply.

calpoly.name: Cal Poly
calpoly.notes: Originator of the CubeSat standard and P-POD deployer.

isis.name: Innovative Solutions In Space (ISIS)
isis.notes: Dutch small satellite manufacturer and launch broker. Flight heritage since 2013.

exolaunch.name: Exolaunch GmbH
exolaunch.notes: German rideshare & integration company; SpaceX Transporter partner.

spaceflight.name: SpaceFlight Inc.
spaceflight.notes: US rideshare aggregator with dedicated orbital transfer vehicles.

rocketlab.name: Rocket Lab
rocketlab.notes: Electron launch vehicle; primarily SSO missions.

alba.name: Alba Orbital
alba.notes: Scottish PocketQube specialist; Pale Blue Dot rideshare missions.

jaxa.name: Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA)
jaxa.notes: ISS-based JEM Small Satellite Orbital Deployer (J-SSOD). Orbit: 51.6° incl, 380–420 km. Deployment from Kibo airlock.

dorbit.name: D-Orbit
dorbit.notes: Italian OTV operator. ION Satellite Carrier provides precise deployment across multiple orbits within a single mission. Launches as secondary payload on Falcon 9, Vega-C, etc.

maverick.name: Maverick Space Systems
maverick.notes: US deployer manufacturer (San Luis Obispo, CA). NASA GEVS-qualified Mercury-series dispensers. Compatible with Firefly Alpha, ABL RS1, and other launch vehicles.
